item.title: Trump holds key White House meeting to make "final determination" on Iran deal
item.description: President Trump said Friday that Iran must agree to never have a nuclear weapon and to reopen the Strait or Hormuz immediately, without tolls.

item.title: Why have tomato prices surged nearly 40% in a year?
item.description: Experts point to several factors, from tariffs to weather, behind the rapid price increase in the humble tomato.

item.title: Judge blocks closure of Kennedy Center, orders removal of Trump's name
item.description: A judge blocked the Kennedy Center from temporarily closing its doors during renovations, and ruled that the institution's board acted unlawfully when it added President Trump's name to the building.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine bracing for "big attacks" by Russia in next 24 hours
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine has received intelligence indicating Russia will launch an assault involving drones and missiles.

item.title: White House has yet to release results of Trump's latest physical
item.description: The White House has yet to release a summary the results of President Trump's latest physical exam.

item.title: Romania says Russian drone hit apartments, lightly wounding 2 people
item.description: The Russian drone struck an apartment building, wounding two people, Romanian officials said.

item.title: Blue Origin assesses impact of New Glenn explosion
item.description: Blue Origin assess the impact of Thursday's New Glenn explosion, prompting concern about NASA moon program delays.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for May 31, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy and former Vice President Mike Pence.

item.title: No tax charges filed in SPLC probe after IRS lawyers found informant program lawful
item.description: Federal agents from FBI and IRS probed the SPLC's paid informant program starting in Trump's first term.

item.title: Medical expert on prostate cancer as Jill Biden describes Joe Biden's "shocking" diagnosis
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden is commenting on former President Joe Biden's "shocking" prostate diagnosis in an interview for "CBS Sunday Morning." Mount Sinai's Dr. Michael Herman joins with more.

item.title: 5 dead, dozens injured as bus plows into 6 vehicles in Virginia
item.description: The five deaths came in vehicles that were struck by the bus when it did not slow down for traffic.

item.title: Jeff Bezos reacts to huge Blue Origin rocket explosion that could impact U.S. moon ambitions
item.description: A Blue Origin rocket exploded during testing before a June launch, a potential setback for NASA as the U.S. plans advancements on the moon. Franklin Institute chief astronomer Derrick Pitts joins CBS News with analysis and more on Jeff Bezos' reaction.

item.title: Shrey Parikh describes winning 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee
item.description: Shrey Parikh from Rancho Cucamonga, California, joins CBS News with his reaction after winning the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $10,000 long-term CD can earn now
item.description: Want to earn a big return on your money for months (or years)? Here's how much you'd make with a $10,000 long-term CD.

item.title: 5 ways to cut your HVAC energy bill without sacrificing comfort
item.description: These small changes to your HVAC habits could help lower your energy costs without making your home uncomfortable.

item.title: How Colombian presidential election will impact U.S. relationship
item.description: Colombians are heading to the polls to elect their next president, who is bound to impact relations with the U.S., a key regional ally. María Teresa Aya with Noticias Caracol joins CBS News with more.

item.title: She waited for a soulmate who never showed up: ChatGPT users detail AI delusions
item.description: AI-fueled delusions can happen when chatbots respond to grandiose, paranoid or imaginary ideas with affirmation or encouragement.

item.title: Pam Bondi testifies behind closed doors in House committee's Epstein probe
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi testified before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.

item.title: Pam Bondi "refused" to answer Trump-related questions while testifying on Epstein, Garcia says
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi was heckled by Epstein abuse survivors before she entered a closed-door interview to testify for House Oversight Committee members about the Justice Department's Epstein probe. CBS News' Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: Air traffic control run by Compaq computers is safe but inefficient, FAA head says
item.description: As the summer travel season starts to take off, FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford tells CBS News he has confidence in the system, despite hundreds of FAA facilities being run on decades-old technology.

item.title: Trump outlines Iran demands before Situation Room meeting on deal
item.description: President Trump posted to social media his demands on the future of Iran as he announced a meeting in the Situation Room to discuss a potential deal. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Eleanor Watson report.

item.title: Southwest revises its overweight passenger rules. Here's what's changing.
item.description: Southwest is walking back some recent changes in its policies for passengers who require a second seat.

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass congressional map designed to pick up GOP seat
item.description: The Louisiana Legislature passed a new congressional map that would leave the state with only one of its two majority-Black districts.

item.title: Trump discussing Iran deal in Situation Room
item.description: President Trump assembled members of his national security team to discuss the latest proposal on Iran. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Eleanor Watson report.

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks DOJ work on $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Justice Department announced the $1.7 billion fund as part of a settlement of a civil lawsuit President Trump brought against the IRS.

item.title: FAA chief reacts to Mullin's suggestion to halt international flights to Newark over ICE protests
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration Administrator Bryan Bedford is reacting to Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin's suggestion to halt international flights to Newark, New Jersey, amid tense ICE protests. Lindsey Reiser reports.

item.title: ICE agent accused of shooting man in north Minneapolis arrested in Texas
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent accused of shooting a man in the leg in north Minneapolis and then lying about the attack​ was arrested in Texas Friday morning, according to the Hennepin County Attorney's Office.

item.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine is bracing for big attacks from Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that his country is bracing for "big attacks" from Russia on Friday or Saturday night. "Our people have to be very, very careful, cautious, and children, and they have to use bomb shelters...nobody knows 100%, but there is a high percent," Zelenskyy said in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: How Shrey Parikh won the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee
item.description: 14-year-old Shrey Parikh from Rancho Cucamonga, California, won the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee. CBS News' Jarred Hill reports.

item.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
item.description: The brutal murders of four teenage girls has haunted Austin, Texas, for 30 years. Could new information lead to a killer? "48 Hours" correspondent Erin Moriarty has reported on the case from the beginning and has the latest on the search for answers. | CASE UPDATE: In September 2025, the Austin Police Department identified Robert Eugene Brashers, a serial killer and rapist, as the suspect in the Texas yogurt shop murders. Brashers, who is deceased, was tied to the murders through DNA testing. On Feb. 19, 2026, a judge declared innocent the four men who were wrongfully accused of the murders — formally exonerating them. In May 2026, the City of Austin agreed to pay a settlement of $35 million to be split among the men.
